sourceMovie.title: Street Kings
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Street Kings (2008) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Havoc
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Street Kings clicked for you, Havoc (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Street Kings?
faq.answer: Try Havoc, Colors, End of Watch—each shares DNA with Street Kings while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Street Kings?
faq.answer: Street Kings (2008) is best described as Action, Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Street Kings part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Street Kings Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
